name: Nicotinamide mononucleotide raised blood NAD metabolites in older men
summary: A small randomized trial supports NMN target engagement in older men, with exploratory muscle-function signals that need replication.
statement: In healthy older men, 250 mg/day nicotinamide mononucleotide increased whole-blood NAD+ and related metabolites; reported gait-speed and left-grip changes were nominal and exploratory.
population_or_model: Healthy Japanese men aged at least 65 years
quantitative_note: Forty-two participants were randomized; a supplement-supply error led the authors to exclude 12-week data for 22 participants from some analyses.
caveats: The functional findings were exploratory and require larger validation.
The trial included only older men.
Some authors were employees of the supplement supplier.
